# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create Linkerd default authorization policy for a namespace. Configure policy to handle services without explicit authorization rules. Set up default behavior for service-to-service communication. This is for our service mesh policy framework.
# Category: linkerd_policy_default_allow

apiVersion: linkerd.io/v1alpha2
kind: AuthPolicy
metadata:
  name: ${NAMESPACE}
spec:
  selector: {} # match all services in the namespace unless otherwise specified here, by selecting them directly or specifying a different policy if desired. Here we select every service because not everyone has an explicit authorization rule set to apply everywhere else (like before). Note that it's best practice for your AuthPolicy resources when using Linkerd Service Mesh policies and this is just one such example
  template: {} # the following configuration will be applied only if no other policy matches. In many cases, you can leave either selector or a non-empty spec unset because usually all services in any namespace are covered by default authorization rules for Linkerd Service Mesh policies provided here with empty templates to cover service-to-service communication defaults and nothing else specified
  outboundRule: {} # the following configuration will be applied only if no other policy matches. Here we do not specify anything because typically, all services in a namespace would have explicit authorization rules set by default for Linkerd Service Mesh policies provided here with empty templates to cover service-to-service communication defaults and nothing else specified
